entry.title: EXIT_SAPLL03A_005 stock removal strategy is overwritten by FIFO
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

I m looking for some support in regards to:

  • Function Module: EXIT_SAPLL03A_005
  • Include Program: ZXLTOU07

I have requested developer to build custom stock removal strategy which would work 2 ways:

Objective

Develop a custom stock removal strategy in SAP Warehouse Management (WM) using the enhancement MWMTO004 (Function Module EXIT_SAPLL03A_005) to:

  1. Primary Goal: Pick the full required quantity from a single bin if available.
  1. Fallback Logic: If no single bin contains the full quantity, apply the standard FIFO strategy to pick stock from multiple bins based on the oldest goods receipt date.

And here we run into the problem after code debug it looks like it is working, but after TR is converted to TO it looks like it applies standard fifo strategy. I have parameters set in prog par, under storage type level stock removal strategy is set to F with user exit check active.

T_QMAT looks correct in this case it picked from the right bin because logic is met.

My question is do we use the right exit ? or we miss some FM to create TO. Code below all we got.

 

Code example:

  DATA:
    lv_enh_applicable TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

  zdovista_par=>get_prog_par_multi( EXPORTING ip_programm = 'ZXLTOU07'
                                              ip_par      = 'USER_EXIT'
                                    IMPORTING et_value    = DATA(lt_lgnum_lgtyp) ).
  LOOP AT lt_lgnum_lgtyp INTO DATA(ls_param).
    SPLIT ls_param-value AT ';' INTO DATA(lv_lgnum) DATA(lv_lgtyp).
    IF i_ltap-lgnum = lv_lgnum AND
        i_ltap-vltyp = lv_lgtyp.
      lv_enh_applicable = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
  "1. Check for Full Quantity in a Single Bin
  IF lv_enh_applicable = abap_true.
    LOOP AT t_qmat ASSIGNING <ls_qmat>
                   WHERE verme >= i_anfme."i_ltap-vsola.
      <ls_qmat>-ausme = i_anfme."i_ltap-vsola
      LOOP AT t_qmat ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_qmat_others>)
                   WHERE lgnum <> <ls_qmat>-lgnum AND
                         lqnum <> <ls_qmat>-lqnum.
        CLEAR: <ls_qmat_others>-ausme.
      ENDLOOP.
      EXIT.
    ENDLOOP.

    "2. Fallback to FIFO Strategy
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      SORT t_qmat BY wdatu ASCENDING.
      DATA(lv_remaining_qty) = i_anfme."i_ltap-vsola.

      LOOP AT t_qmat ASSIGNING <ls_qmat>.
        IF <ls_qmat>-verme >= lv_remaining_qty.
          <ls_qmat>-ausme = lv_remaining_qty.
          EXIT.
        ELSE.
          <ls_qmat>-ausme = <ls_qmat>-verme.
          lv_remaining_qty = lv_remaining_qty - <ls_qmat>-verme.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

entry.title: purchase order report that includes custom fields and project ID
entry.content:
Hi expert

May I ask how to extract a purchase order report that includes custom fields and project ID?

I have created a custom field in the purchase document for reporting and analysis purposes. However, this custom field is not available in the "Monitor Purchase Order Items" app. I would like to find a report or app that allows me to view the custom field together with the project ID, along with basic purchase order information (PO number, supplier, item, quantity, net price, and net amount).

Does anyone know if there is an app that can display this information?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP PPDS Tank Planning Batch production
entry.content:
It seems to be a process industry. Try to model using a recipe. Consider maintaining Start-to-Start (SS) relationship between the filling and production phases. Set the consumption type to 'C' in the Product Storage Definition (PSD). Resting time can be in same recipe. Maintain Draining as first operation in the higher level (FG?) BOM recipe with a Finish-to-Start (FS) relationship between resting and draining phase. Resource for resting and draining is same in this case, but in different recipes.

1. PI01 control key can be used.
2. Configuring storage location is mandatory and must be assigned to the tank resource. If you want the system to automatically generate the PSD during Product Data Structure (PDS) generation, go to the SPRO transaction /SAPAPO/TPCUST and enable the "Generate Production Storage Definition During PDS Generation" option. While consumption types can be set to default in same configuration, I prefer to maintain them manually in /SAPAPO/STORDEF for better tank wise accuracy 

Other fields in resource master to consider - Under the capacity header, the available capacity in terms of volume and quantity fields, Storage characteristics and Resource category = 'Warehouse'.

After planned order generation, check the result in fill chart - detailed scheduling board.

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content:
I often get personal messages asking for support outside the forum, and while I do enjoy helping, it can be frustrating.
I'm not available 24/7, and I prefer not to provide solutions via email, since that only helps one person. The whole point of this community is to share knowledge so everyone can benefit.

entry.title: The 'Manage Document Output History' Report does not display any data in Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Despite selecting various views and filters, no data appears in the 'Manage Document Output History' report when executed.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The 'Manage Document Output History' Report does not...
entry.content:
The required Work Center Views are not assigned to the employee.

As a key user, you can perform these actions only if the following Work Center Views are assigned to the user:

OUTPUT_RETRY_CUST_INV: This work center view allows you to resubmit or retry Customer Invoice documents from the report.

OUTPUT_RETRY_SUPP_INV: This work center view allows you to resubmit or retry Supplier Invoice documents from the report.

OUTPUT_RETRY_DELIVERY_NOTES: This work center view allows you to resubmit or retry Delivery Notes from the report.

OUTPUT_RETRY_GOODS_SRV_RCPT: This work center view allows you to resubmit or retry Goods and Services Receipts from the report.

Please, assign above Work Center Views to the employee in order to access 'Manage Document Output History' Report.

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload in Fiori Elements List Report Using Guid...
entry.content:
Hi,

From Console to check if any "event handler not found" messages appear when you click 'Begin' or 'Cancel'
In the extended controller, hope mapping is correctly in place:-

async onOpenDialog() {
			this.oDialog ??= await this.loadFragment({
				name: "ui5.walkthrough.view.HelloDialog"
			});

			this.oDialog.open();
		},

		onCloseDialog() {
			// note: We don't need to chain to the pDialog promise, since this event-handler
			// is only called from within the loaded dialog itself.
			this.byId("helloDialog").close();
		}

Please refer: Walkthrough - Step 17 - Fragment Callbacks - Samples - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

entry.title: Documentation about EWM datasources (0WM_DLVI and 0WM_WO)
entry.content:
Hello,
 
I am trying to implement Business Content for EWM integration between S/4HANA and BW/4HANA. I am encountering issues with the two datasources 0WM_DLVI and 0WM_WO, which seem to function similarly to 2LIS extractors.
 
I found SAP Note 3545479, which explains that the table /SCWM/TBWACT needs to be updated in SPRO to define the events relevant for BW and the update mode. However, after two days of searching on Google, the SAP Community, and SAP Support, I have only found the following post, which includes some screenshots of the documentation:
Link to SAP Community post.
 
Could you help me locate the official documentation to understand how to configure these data flows? Specifically, I am looking for information on steps such as deleting the setup table, scheduling jobs for data collection, and other related tasks.
 
I have also been unable to find a document that appeared to exist several years ago:
https://wiki.scn.sap.com/wiki/display/SCM/How+to+Integrate+EWM+with+BW
 
I only have the screenshot below:

 

 

Thank you for your assistance,
Franck

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @AndreasW ,

the SAP DPG is a convenience feature that differentiates to your described points:

  • Necessary aretefacts are created automatically
  • The data is stored in Objectstore and not in HANA
  • Data sharing as DataProduct e.g. towards DBX is only possible via BDC

Best Regards

Christof

entry.title: Create_by_url : Issue in patch call from SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Patch method working fine in postman with same body and same url with same credentials

But i am calling through SAP by using create_by_url and getting response as 500 error code internal server error.

data : lo_http  type ref to if_http_client.

          lv_url type string,

          lv_body type string

          lv_length type i.

lv_url = https://desk-*******************************************************.

TRY.

      cl_http_client->create_by_url(

            EXPORTING

                  url    = lv_url

                  ssl_id = 'ANONYM'

            IMPORTING

                 client   = lo_http

            EXCEPTIONS

                  argument_not_fount = 1

                  plugin_not_active = 2

                 internal_error     = 3

                others    = 4 ).

      Catch cx_root into data(lo_root).

ENDTRY.

lo_http->propertytype_logon_popup = lo_http->disabled.

CALL METHOD lo_http->authencicate

         EXPORTING 

                    username = XXXXX

                    password  = xxxxxxxxx.

* Patch method set

CALL METHOD lo_http->request->set_header_field

    EXPORTING 

           name =  '~request_method'

           value = 'PATCH' .

* Content type

CALL METHOD lo_http->request->set_header_field

    EXPORTING

             name = 'Content-Type'

             value = 'application/json'

*Prepare Body

CONCATENATE '[ {"op": "replace" . "path": "fieldname****" , "value": "XX" }]' into lv_body.

lv_length = strlen (lv_body).

* SET body

CALL METHOD lo_http->request->set_cdata

    EXPORTING 

         data = lv_body

        offset = 0

       length = lv_length .

CALL METHOD lo_http->send

       EXCEPTIONS

             http_communication_failure = 1

             http_invalid_state = 2.

CALL METHOD lo_http->receive

    EXCEPTIONS 

        http_communication_failure = 1

       http_invalid_state = 2

      http_processing_failed = 3.

data : lv_code type i,

         lv_reason type string.

lo_http->response->get_status(

Impoarting 

code = lv_code

reason = lv_reason ).

lo_http->close( ). 

____________________________________________________

Getting lv_code as 500 and lv_reason as Internal server error.

I have used get method also that is working fine with same credentials.

Is there any place where i am missing something

(Only difference i am setting 'RAW' and type 'JSON' in postman)

entry.title: Re: CR reports cannot connect to SQL Server database
entry.content:
Well that makes no sense??? You said it just stopped working, So nothing CR did caused this, something in your environment is the cause. Did you try what I suggested above?

Enable DB Logging and send me the log, I'll go through it and see if I can see the reason why?

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1603398

One thing to be aware of, CR uses memory to load the Database info into memory to show the DB structures etc. IF you go over that limit nothing shows up, this may explain why you don't see anything now... Your SQL Server DB structure is getting large...

On the connection property select Options, you can add filtering, try unchecking everything except Tables, try each one, if that doesn't show anything then try adding filtering to see if that helps.

As for support you have direct link to SAP R&D through me, but so far you have not provided any info to send to the Developers to see what is happening.

You just gave up, if you can't prove it's a CR issue there's nothing CR can do to help you.

Saying you can't get a SAP Support Engineer to assist is a lame excuse...

Good luck

The requirement is to send each project record individually to a third-party REST API (3rd Party HRMS), which only accepts one record per request in application/x-www-form-urlencoded format with the key name inputData.

I tried using Iterating Splitter, but I am not getting the expected behavior — it's not making separate HTTP calls or invoking the Request Reply as intended.

My goal is:

  • Fetch XML containing multiple projects

  • Split it into individual messages (each project)

  • Send one-by-one using a Request Reply step to the API

  • Handle and log each response if needed

    Any step-by-step help, example flow, or blog reference would be highly appreciated.

    Thanks in advance! 
    Best regards,
    Prathmesh Sutar

entry.title: Change/Update APM Cloud Connector
entry.content:
1. Can you change the Cloud Connector in BTP that is used for the connectivity between our S/4 system and SAP APM?
  2. Are there any limitations?
  3. Does this have impact on the APM tenant and the running integration?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change/Update APM Cloud Connector
entry.content:
1. Can you change the Cloud Connector in BTP that is used for the connectivity between our S/4 system and SAP APM?
    Yes, you can change/update the cloud connector configuration/settings

  2. Are there any limitations?
    No

  3. Does this have impact on the APM tenant and the running integration?
    Only if the connection is deactivated/deleted will the sync between S/4 and APM not happen but once the connection is active/re-created the sync will take place between S/4 and APM.

  4. Would changing the cloud connector impact the event mesh in anyway?
    It will not impact event mesh.

entry.title: Re: Procurement Overview Page
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

Please, note that Procurement overview app encompasses several apps, that need to be added to your custom role in order to work too. Hence, please, add the target mappings going to the following apps, as you did by referencing the standard catalog for procurement overview, to get the corresponding mini tiles in Procurement Overview app:

Monitor purchase contracts: PurchaseContractItem-monitor

Manage purchase contracts: PurchaseContract-manage

Monitor purchase requisition items: PurchaseRequisitionItem-monitor

Monitor purchase order items: PurchaseOrderItem-monitorPurDocItems

Purchasing Spend: PurchaseOrder-analyzePurchasingSpend

Non-Managed Spend: PurchaseOrder-analyzeNonManagedSpend

Off-contract spend; PurchaseOrder-analyzeOffContractSpend

Purchase requisition item types: PurchaseRequisitionItem-analyzeItemTypes

Purchase requisition no touch rate: PurchaseRequisitionItem-analyzeNoTouchRate

Monitor supplier confirmations: PurchaseOrderItem-monitorSupplierConfirmations

 

Also, please, make sure that the previous apps are configured well according to fiori library and add the required authorizations to your user. 

 

 Thanks and regards, 

 

Maria

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: BODS: Integrity constraint violation error with Table comparison in SAP Data Services
entry.content:
Dear All,

we have BODS 4.3 and we load data from Postgresql AWS source DB to Datasphere (we recently move the target from Hana on-premise to Datasphere). 

Some delta loads end in error with the classic message: Integrity constraint violation, but the incomprehensible thing is that between source and destination there is a table comparison step that compares, by key, all the columns of the table. Shouldn't the system automatically upsert the records?Nothing changes by selecting or deselecting the 'bulk load' flag. We have solved it with the autocorrect load flag but this slows down the data loading.

Do you have any idea about this issue?

Thanks and regards

Luca

entry.title: Estimating SAP PO To BTP-IS Migration - Get It Right The First time
entry.content:
In SAP the best run projects are the one's that have been best scoped!

No one likes surprises and Change requests are fairly common in projects where the estimation wasn't done correctly due to uncertainty  in the initially scoped requirements or technical requirements which could have been pre-empted at an earlier stage and catered for in the budget.

SAP PO To BTP migration projects aren't immune to such un-foreseen changes either but such changes can be reduced by a great margin by estimating  the project right.


Let me start with an analogy of a house. If you have built a house or are going to build a house you often come across varying construction costs / quotes from different builders for strikingly similar designs, look and feel. House A might cost you 200k$ and House B 400$ and House C 600$.

One may ask what is the difference? 

The difference lies in the inclusions offered in the house! I am not sure about other geographies but in Australia if someone is building a house they get quotes from different builders and then compare the inclusions (which sometimes go on for more than 20 -30 pages ) line by line. 

RFP's are quite similar where customers go through the features offered / assumed technical requirements and the cost and then decide the outcome. But what if neither you or the customer are aware of the missed requirements / gaps which should be addressed as part of the estimation? This results in change requests. (another reason being new requirements).

Coming back to the house analogy, the difference can also lie in 

FeatureHouse A ($200k)House B ($400K)House C ($600k)
FrameTimberSteelTimber
SecurityNoneCCTV, AlarmNone
Water ConnectionNoneYesYes
ElectricityNoneYesNone

As you can see in the comparison above it doesn't matter what the cost is, what matters more is - What is included! What is on the offer! What are the standard features being offered in the proposal.

House A - though cheaper would need multiple CR's which will bring the cost up as its missing the technical requirements.

House B - Best Suited to the budget, fair pricing

House C - The builder has a office on wall street. 

To avoid situations where projects overshoot their budgets its absolutely necessary to understand what are the salient features of robust integrations and what should be covered in good migration proposal.

SAP's Migration Assessment Report -   What is covered and What is not!

SAP's migration assessment report clearly states that the estimates only include Technical migration efforts, To give you an idea of what they don't include which should be and will be a part of the Migration see this comparison.

We will discuss these briefly but there are a few more things that you would never know if you haven't done a few migrations yourself and I discovered these over the last few years.

1) That you really cant migrate your update_insert jdbc scenarios from SAP PO to Delete and Insert in SAP CI (batch) or even to update_insert that kills the performance. You have to rely on Stored Procedures.

2) For SELECT / UPDATE operations SAP CI jdbc adapter doesn't behave in the same manner as SAP PO JDBC adapter. You do SELECT and UPDATE in different calls to the DB server and you run the risk of updating the flag for more entries than you selected again you should rely on stored procs.

3) Another interesting find was that SOAP RM adapter has a limit of 50k records both inbound and outbound which can be increased on request by SAP. therefore you should either limit your reports/ programs in SAP to select / push less than the number of records you have set for your tenant OR increase SAP CI Tenants record limit from 50K records per SOAP message to a really large number lets say 300k records but then run the risk of crossing the threshold of 100 mb message size which most of have (configurable) for outbound webservice / proxy message size sent via http out of S4.

4) You can convert your RFC/proxy scenarios to soamanager webservices (consumer proxies / service definitions) leaving IDOC scenarios as is.

5) For NFS scenarios you can either convert them to outbound async Webservices / XML or you can replace that NFS with a SFTP server.

6) Message Mappings: Don't trust the SAP Migration tool blindly when it comes to Message mappings. Did you know that SAP Migration tool OVERWRITES the schema / WSDL file if the Message types in Source and Target structure have same name even if they belong to different SWCV?
Impact: The impact is that even if the names are same the structure is most of the times different and the message mapping basically gets corrupted and needs manual fix which is to export and import the source or target schema manually. Even if structures are same a misplaced namespace can cause the message to fail in SAP S4 or in third party system.

7) Migrating proxies to MDR: Do you know that proxies sometime or most of the time break as soon as you convert inline types to global types in ESR? This needs substantial manual effort Integration + ABAP if you have large number of proxies. always estimate that upfront.

😎 SAP PO Migration tool converts Message types to XSD's which are not editable in SAP CI - Graphically. Import your data types Message types separately if you need them.

9) Proxies vs SOAP RM : If you are doing a big bang and are running low on time proxy is fine, you can keep the XI adapters as XI, what this does is it forces the proxy connection to SAP CI over a single RFC destination, which is okay as long as you dont plan to run anything through SAP PO. BUT if you are doing a phased go live re-evaluate your approach as you would have to have SAP_PROXY_ESR pointed to SAP PO for the interfaces that get left behind and the only solution would be to have SOAP RM set up in soamanager and replace XI with SOAP RM which doesn't take more than 5 minutes.

10) XSLT / UDF: It's not just UDF's that need to be rebuilt, a lot of time consultants use Dynamic Configuration in XSLT mappings which is kind of redundant in SAP CI, given we have properties but such XSLT's almost never work in SAP CI and need modification.

11) SAP Migration tool inserts blank/empty exception handling subprocesses in each integration process for an iflow which is just a nuisance and serves no real purpose.

12) you cannot migrate a ICO with multiple operations in the ICO to SAP CI IFlow using the SAP Migration tool.

13) XPATH based routing conditions in SAP PO ICO dont work the same way in SAP CI, it might be CPATH version difference but I have seen it first hand, never trust the XPATH based conditions for receiver determination in SAP CI if it has not been tested properly.

14) XSLT Mappings dont work in SAP CI as is if you have dynamic configuration embedded into them

15) Migration tool should be capable of creating credentials in security store for scenarios where credentials are stored in the channel config in SAP PO but it's not hence it's an additional task that needs to be done.

16) migration tool shouldn't convert the message types to WSDL's  as customers can now no longer maintain them graphically , SAP should either provide a functionality to modify the WSDL/XSD or should import the types as DDT and MT's. The SI should import these to SAP CI in separate packages to avoid cluttering. 

17) Be extra careful with BPM scenarios, Sometimes the ICO might look very simple and straight forward but you will in most of the cases have to re-deisgn the entire thing which may take days or weeks depending upon the complexity. 

18) SI's should take PO to BTP Migration as an opportunity to modernize Inbound and Outbound authentication mechanisms , like moving from Basic auth to Certificate Based Auth. If there is no restriction in terms of cost do not use SAP provided certificates for third party / Outbound cert based Authentication but get new CA signed certificates with a longer validity to spare yourself frequent downtimes.

19) Queues Queues and Queues! JMS queues are your best friend use them wherever they fit - asynchronous integrations. 

20 ) Define a solid strategy for using your Dev SAP CI tenant as both a Test and Dev Tenant and for connecting to two / three systems.

21) Modularize re-usable parts of integrations and EXTERNALIZE your Parameters!

22) Approach third party systems/vendors on day 1 to avoid any delays later on and get a commitment and resources allocated for testing support.   

23) Did you know that migrating aa proxy to MDR can take anywhere between 10 minute to  4 hours! Yes for a single proxy. For migrating a proxy to MDR you need to ensure that all the data types are global types and not inline types. Depending on the size of the schema it may take 10 minutes to 2 hours to convert the types and then surprise surprise! Your Re-generated proxy doesn't work anymore! Wasn't expecting that right. We then need to adjust the proxies and retest them thoroughly for an bugs.  

As you can see a SAP Po to BTP Migration involves extensive planning and a very Strong Integration Architect to drive the transformation. 

An SI should factor all these into the estimates and depending on the partner you are choosing the costs and the quality can vary a lot from vendor to vendor. Also It's not only about the quality of build / processes that drives a transformation to success. The most important thing that's often missed in such transformations is how approachable your team is and how much attention you are paying on building trust and how transparent you are.

Let me know your thoughts and if there are any learnings you came across worth capturing here.

entry.title: Re: On-premise SNC SAPCryptolib with publicly trusted CA...
entry.content:
Right, this time I will answer my own question.

Firstly, apologies to SAP for saying it was an oversight because I did not realise this was possible.

Having seen all the various examples around that were all using self-signed certificates for the SNC PSE I thought this was the only way. I thought wrong. My solution was to simply use my SAPSSLS.pse from SSL server standard already containing a trusted signed CA and import that as my SNC PSE. I also had to set my snc/identity/as parameter to use the same suffix as the trusted certificate. I added the SAP Cloud Root certificate to this in STRUST. I left the SU01 SNC user pattern the same as the cloud.

This means I no longer need to worry about distributing my own self-signed CA and will just renew the SNC certificate whenever I do the same for the SSL standard which it can do in-place.

Let me know if you see any issue with this approach but I will mark it as answered.

entry.title: How to handle multilevel inter-company for service contract in public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We have a requirement to support an intercompany service contract process for capacity-based services.

As per my understanding, the single-level intercompany scenario can be handled using a Royalties Intercompany Condition Contract setup. However, in our case, we need to support a multi-level intercompany chain.

Scenario Example:

  • The end customer is located in the USA.

  • The USA entity routes the request to the Netherlands entity.

  • The Netherlands entity does not have the service available, so it forwards the request to Luxembourg.

  • The Luxembourg entity provides the service directly to the end customer.

Our Requirement:

We need to understand how this multi-tier intercompany service fulfillment process can be modeled in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (or On-Prem if applicable), especially in the context of:

  • Service contract flow

  • Intercompany billing and cost flow across all three entities

  • Revenue recognition and profitability tracking

  • Legal entity compliance

I would greatly appreciate any guidance or recommendations on the best practice approach or supported architecture for such a scenario.

Thanks & Best regards,
Tirupati RK

entry.title: Re: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content:
We're doing smth. similar and it's working 

 

let row = await SELECT.one.from(MyEntity.drafts).where({ 'ID': req.params[0].ID }).columns(['DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID', 'value'])
let draftID = row.DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID

languages.forEach(element => {
                
    texts.push({ 'ID': req.params[0].ID, 'ID_texts': uuid(), 'locale': element.code, 'value': found.text, 'DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID': draftID });

}

// Update db with translated texts
await INSERT.into(MyEntity.texts.drafts).entries(texts);

Btw. You do not need a custom controller.

entry.title: Re: How to automatically create a Job Information event a...
entry.content:
Hi William,
Can you describe how the termination and garden leave process is currently handled and recorded? Specifically, how is the "Garden Leave date" field used in the Termination portlet — is it manually populated?
As you know, there is a record created in Job Information when a termination reason and termination date are saved in Employment Details, and that's a possible reason why the cross-entity rule doesn't create another event in Job Info when saving termination with garden leave date.
What I understand from you is that the garden leave date is different from the termination date, and you aim to create an event in Job Information based on this garden leave date.
See below SAP knowledge article about cross-entity rules from Employment Details to understand the capabilities and limitations of this functionality. Maybe this will help in finding a solution for your scenario.
https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/implementing-and-managing-employment-lifecycle-from-hiring-to-termination/business-rule-handling-for-employment-details

entry.title: Bug with Opening Task in Calendar - SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing some bugs when using the calendar for planning workflows. The problem is the following: we have implemented a workflow process where two users are owners of all the workflow,  “DMORATILLA and “BDUENAS”.

The problem is that when Diego is the one opening and cancelling the workflow, it works fine, but if Beatriz cancels and reopens the workflow, then it doesn't work. We don't know what's causing this behavior to happen. Here are some evidences:

The workflow doesn’t work when:

  1. Diego cancels the workflow.
  2. Beatriz opens the workflow.

In this case the second step a data blocking step “data”, doesn’t work and the error that is showing is the next one:

This error is not possible, since the data lock step should be running automatically because Diego is the owner and the assignee for the task as well as the data locking owner inside the model dimensions.

And here are the permissions for the data locking driving dimensions:

The workflow works when:

  1. Diego cancels the process.
  2. Diego opens the process. 

The workflow works correctly, and it doesn’t ask for any permission.

 

Any help is welcome, king regards,

Alejandro.

entry.title: Unable to add the difference in the foreach.booked with each product in SAP SAC Data action
entry.content:
Hi All,

Here is the scenario we are facing the issues, we have to read each record by record and calculate the sub total and main total also should remain same even after re-allocation at discrete level.

Measure used is a Volume ,it cannot be a decimal.

we have some pre-populated volumes from source for PNO12 with sub -totals as 45, 9 and 27 respectively.

Now user ,will re-allocate the volumes from existing customer category to a new categories like 

10A = 30 , 20A = 30 and 71A = 21, after the allocation sub totals should remain same at CC and PNO12

like 45 at 224 H1 G3 0 D 1 19 and so on.

 

Please correct this below code and suggest if any idea and it should be dynamic.

CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY =[d/DIM_BPACCT]
CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES =ON
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] ="ZVOLUME"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_DATASRC]="CALCULATE"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/Cutoff_Date] TO [d/Version].[p/Enddate]
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_MKT] = BASEMEMBER([d/DIM_MKT] , %UNMarket%)
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_PNO3]= BASEMEMBER([d/DIM_PNO3] ,%PNO3% )
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_VERSION]=%PlanningVersion%
//MEMBERSET [d/DIM_PNO12] =("224 H1 G3 0 D 1 19", "224 H4 G3 0 D 1 19")
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total_Amount OF  [d/Measures]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #STORE6520_COPY OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total6520_INPUT OF [d/Measures]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #CCPercentage OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store_6520INPUT OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total_6520 OF [d/DIM_BPACCT]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #Subtotal_of_Each_CC OF [d/DIM_PNO12]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Difference_in_CC OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION OF  [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
 
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST1 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST2 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST3 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST4 OF [d/Measures]
 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store2 OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #TOTALOFPREVIOUS OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Account OF [d/DIM_BPACCT]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #CC OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
VARIABLEMEMBER #SubTotatofPNO12 OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
VARIABLEMEMBER #CCtotal OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #FINDDIFF OF  [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #VOLSTORE OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #FINALSTORE OF [d/Measures] 
INTEGER @count
INTEGER @CurrentCount
INTEGER @GETTOTAL
 
//Simple copy from 6520_COPY to store it in Variable member
DATA( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#STORE6520_COPY ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME")
 
//Store User Input values 6520_INPUT Example: 10A-27 and 20A-27
DATA([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT" ) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT")
 
//Subtotal of Each PNO12 Example: 45 , 9 (Account: 6520_COPY)
DATA( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12 ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME")
 
 
//Store user inputted values example, 27+27 = 54 will get here (Account: 6520_INPUT)
DATA([d/Measures]=#Total6520_INPUT, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CCtotal,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )
         = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#")
 
 
//Find the Percentage for user Inputted values Example: 27/54, 27/54 (Account: 6520_INPUT)  
DATA([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" ) =( RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#")/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Total6520_INPUT, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CCtotal,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" ))
 
//Multiply Percentage with PNO12 Subtotal Example: 0.5*45, 0.5*45  AND 0.5*9 , 0.5*9  (Account: 6520_COPY)//
//Volume will be 22, 22 AND 4, 4 due to integer measure
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )*RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)
 
//Store the same calculation into variable member to get the decimal values (Original allocated values,Example: 22.5, 22.5 AND 4.5, 4.5)
DATA([d/Measures]=#VOLSTORE, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )*RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)
 
 
//PNO12 Subtotal after allocation Example: After allocation 22+22-->44 and  4+4-->8 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
 
//Each CC subtotal after allocation, Example : 10A=22+4->26 , 20A=22+4-->26
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
//Find difference between Pre Allocated and Post Allocated... 10A(PreAlloc) - 10A(PostAlloc)
DATA([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT",[d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#")- RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
 
 
 
//1st PNO12
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
 
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
 
@count=@Count+1
IF @count=1 THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION) THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT",[d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#")  THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDFOR
ENDIF
 
 
 
//2nd PNO12
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST1, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST2, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC)= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
 
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
@count=@Count+1
//FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
IF @count=2 THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST2, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC) THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST1, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID]) THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
//ENDFOR
ENDFOR
ENDIF
 
 
//3rd PNO12
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST3, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST4, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC)= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
 
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
@count=@Count+1
//FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
IF @count=3 THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST4, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC) THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST3, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID]) THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDFOR
//ENDFOR
ENDIF

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content:
I just blocked private messages because of these requests. All these people don't understand what a community is, how to build it, they just want to be helped personally (as we can see, people abandoning their question after getting an answer, or just answering "I solved myself" or "solved", i.e. no feedback to the community). Some of these people have good reasons of course.

They can be educated though. Thank you, community champions, to have the courage to tell them individually to ask their question publicly.

entry.title: Re: HANA ODBC Test Connection Error
entry.content: If the other suggestions such as checking the (allow list) list of IPs that are allowed to connect to your SAP HANA Cloud instance are configured correctly (configured in SAP HANA Cloud Central), verifying that you are using a recent version of the SAP HANA Client (current version is 2.24),  and looking at a trace, you might wish to try connecting using some different tools such as the SQL Console and dbisql which are described in the following tutorial mission.  https://developers.sap.com/mission.hana-cloud-clients.html

entry.title: Request for Guidance on Material Loan Regularization in SAP
entry.content:
We are currently facing a challenge in implementing a material loan process in SAP. These materials are typically semi finished goods used in the production process.

The process is that we have relationship with a third party or external company and we receive materials from them and then return the same materials at a later date.

This process is currently being handled outside of SAP. 

Given this, I wanted to reach out and ask if anyone has experience implementing or managing a similar process successfully within SAP.

Any insights, best practices, transaction codes, or suggestions for configuration would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: How to load Plan Via flexible upload for 12 months at the same time in group reporting
entry.content:
Hi All,

S4HANA Public Cloud Version

We wanted to know how to load plan data in group reporting for 12 months in one go as we have monthly planning, How is this handled.

Steps followed:

1. Create Budget/Plan Versions

2. Define Consolidation Cycle

3. Assign Consolidation Cycle to Version

4. Bundle remaining period in Data monitor

5. Upload the data through flat file- we are notable to upload data for all 12 months.

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content:
Hello
I also receive private messages in the SAP Community and in Linkedin. People probably see that we are answering many questions and they approach us privately because they don't want appear publicly asking a question, or because it is easier to ask questions in the chat. 

If it's a question from Linkedin, I always explain politely that I'm an SAP employee, and that I should use official SAP channels to support customers and partners, and also that the question should be posted in the forum, so that it becomes a reference for other consultants with the same question in the future, and other consultants can also answer. 

I can solve many issues because I worked a long time in product support, but I don't know the answers of ALL questions (unfortunately 🙂 ), so it's always good to have more people providing the inputs and answering a technical question. 
Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Today's Puzzle: Why is SAP not in the Gartner 2025 FCCS Magic Quadrant?
entry.content:
The Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation (FCCS) was released in March 25, 2025, and, as in years’ past, SAP is not included.

“But surely SAP has a solution for FCCS? Why is SAP not participating?”

Simply put, SAP is not included because SAP is an ERP Suite solution provider, and not a standalone FCCS solution.

Typically, Gartner’s FCCS evaluates solutions that offer a wider range of cloud-based capabilities like financial close management, consolidation, and financial reporting.

SAP customers can benefit from SAP solutions optimized for their data, processes, and landscape:

  • Cloud ERP core capabilities for record to report address the processing steps required to create an entity-level financial report. Data is validated at various points in the process and reconciled by design, with full traceability from the financial report to the underlying financial transaction – and even back to the logistical source – like a sales or purchase order.

This core functionality can be extended if your company needs to manage additional requirements related to multiple geographies, systems, or complex company structures:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing is an orchestration hub that coordinates and automates closing tasks in a single-system or multi-system environment. It provides prescriptive automation content for the required closing steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud – but companies can extend these steps to cover manual tasks or tasks performed in different systems. Additional automation to cover specific media or systems gaps, or very complex workflow, can be added with a no-code / low-code approach using SAP BTP’s Process Automation capabilities.
  • SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting automates the financial consolidation routines required to collect data from all of the entities in the group, manages complex consolidation tasks (currency translations, matrix hierarchies, intercompany eliminations, investment consolidations, etc) and generates financial reports – now with (new!) AI-support for summarization and financial analysis

There are several advantage to using these SAP solutions to meet your FCCS requirements. You can

  • avoid extra reconciliation tasks and risks and delays associated with ETL processes,
  • enforce data quality checks and validations of balances on various financial positions and other managerial dimensions such as cost object, profit center or segment,
  • identify and correct issues at the source – since you’re always working in one environment, you do not have to re-load data when there are adjustments
  • perform tasks before the close – tasks like goods and invoice receipt reconciliation or intercompany can be done on a continuous basis during the period, taking the pressure off the period-end close
  • drill-through auditability for greater visibility and trust – from the highest level consolidated group revenue number, you can drill back to an individual sales order in a specific country,
  • share a user experience across accounting transaction processing, entity close, and group reporting – with the re-use of configurations.

While the vast majority of SAP customers are using these SAP solutions in the SAP Business Suite context, there are some companies that have deployed a “sidecar” approach with AFC and Group Reporting in a cloud system - separate from their financial accounting. This is enabled by SAP Group Reporting’s data loading capabilities (SAP Group Reporting Data Collection ) which allow data to be loaded in a controlled way for entities with accounting data in a separate system.

This setup offers some advantages for specific customer segments, such as immediate access to innovations and SAP’s AI capabilities and roadmap (while their accounting system may be on premise and subject to longer upgrade cycles). However, the advantages that SAP Business Suite integrated-stack customers report in terms of “continuous accounting” and “immediate insight of group-level performance during the period” are not achievable.

Want to learn more? Check out our sap.com pages!

entry.title: Re: Edit Relational Connections in CMC for SAP BO BI 4.3

entry.title: How Agentic AI Is Transforming SAP Process Mining
entry.content:
Introduction

Process Mining has emerged as a vital tool in the modern enterprise toolkit—especially for SAP-driven organizations. It allows businesses to visualize and analyze how processes are actually executed, based on real-time system logs rather than assumptions or design blueprints.

Now, with the rise of Agentic AI—autonomous, decision-capable entities—and Generative AI, which enhances insights with contextual intelligence, the field of Process Mining is undergoing a profound shift. These technologies are enabling not only deeper visibility but also proactive and intelligent process improvement.

This article explores how Process Mining works in SAP landscapes and how AI agents are redefining its capabilities.

Understanding SAP Process Mining

At its core, Process Mining involves extracting and analyzing event logs from systems like SAP ERP. These logs contain timestamped records of activities such as sales order creation, goods receipt, or invoice posting. Through analysis, businesses can reconstruct the actual flow of operations—revealing inefficiencies, rework, and variations that may otherwise go unnoticed.

Unlike traditional process analysis methods, Process Mining offers a data-driven view of how work actually happens—not how it was intended to.

Key Components

  1. Event Logs: Digital breadcrumbs from SAP systems, capturing every transaction and user interaction.
  2. Process Discovery: Visualizes the end-to-end “as-is” flow, often revealing unexpected paths and bottlenecks.
  3. Conformance Checking: Compares actual performance to expected or designed workflows, flagging compliance issues.
  4. Process Enhancement: Enables targeted improvements by identifying inefficiencies and process deviations.

In the SAP ecosystem, these logs typically come from modules like MM (Materials Management), SD (Sales and Distribution), or FI (Financial Accounting).

From Traditional to Agentic Process Mining

The introduction of Agentic AI changes the Process Mining landscape in two key ways:

  • Autonomous Agents: These AI entities can monitor business processes continuously, identify deviations, and even trigger corrective workflows—all without human intervention.
  • Context-Aware Insights: With the help of Generative AI, raw log data is transformed into natural language summaries, predictive insights, and simulation-based recommendations.

This means that instead of a process analyst manually reviewing dashboards, an AI agent could proactively inform stakeholders that "invoice posting delays have increased by 18% this week" and recommend remediation actions.

How SAP Process Mining Works – Step by Step

  1. Data Extraction: Pull event logs from SAP systems like S/4HANA or ECC.
  2. Preprocessing: Clean and structure data—removing inconsistencies or incomplete logs.
  3. Process Discovery: Reconstruct and visualize actual process paths.
  4. Diagnostics & Analysis: Identify bottlenecks, rework, delays, and violations.
  5. Recommendations: With GenAI, generate reports, simulate improvements, or forecast outcomes.
  6. Continuous Monitoring: AI agents can be deployed to watch processes in real time, escalating anomalies or enforcing policies.

Use Cases in SAP Environments

Procure-to-Pay (P2P)

Common P2P challenges—such as blocked invoices, approval delays, or vendor issues—can be highlighted through Process Mining. With Agentic AI, the system can go further: suggesting vendor switches, raising procurement alerts, or modifying routing rules autonomously.

Order-to-Cash (O2C)

In O2C scenarios, AI-enhanced mining identifies late deliveries, duplicate orders, or payment delays. Generative AI can simulate alternate fulfillment strategies or customer segmentation-based workflows to improve cycle times.

Quantifiable Benefits

  • Full Transparency: Real-time visualizations of how your SAP processes actually run.
  • Increased Efficiency: Identify and eliminate steps that add no value.
  • Improved Compliance: Detect and respond to deviations from SOPs and policies.
  • Lower Operational Costs: Reduce manual work, rework, and process delays.
  • Faster Decision-Making: Use AI copilots for contextual, real-time recommendations.

The Rise of Continuous, Agent-Led Monitoring

Traditional Process Mining offered snapshots—Agentic AI turns it into a live feed.

Imagine an AI system constantly scanning your SAP business events. When it spots an abnormal delay in goods receipt confirmations across a plant, it not only highlights it but also:

  • Diagnoses the cause (e.g., vendor inconsistency, approval backlog).
  • Suggests possible resolutions.
  • Triggers automated workflows or alerts the responsible stakeholder.

This kind of “intelligent observer” is what Agentic AI enables.

A Subtle Shift: AI in SAP Transformation Tools

Some modern platforms are integrating these capabilities directly into their process intelligence modules. For example, tools like KTern.AI are embedding AI agents that go beyond just mining logs—they diagnose, act, and learn iteratively.

Each of the following analytics becomes significantly more powerful when backed by Generative AI summarization and Agentic task orchestration, which ensure proactive alerts, automated follow-ups, and human-in-the-loop actions:

  1. Business Process Steps: Provides a detailed breakdown of each step involved in a business process, helping to identify areas for improvement.
  2. Average End-to-End (E2E) Process Duration: Calculates the average time taken for a process to complete from start to finish, setting benchmarks and identifying processes that take longer than expected.
  3. Top Business User: Identifies the users who are most actively involved in the business process, aiding in optimizing workload distribution and identifying potential bottlenecks.
  4. Top Process Step: Highlights the most frequently executed step in the process, which can be a target for automation or optimization.
  5. Process Step with the Most Rework: Identifies the process step that requires the most revisions or repetitions, indicating inefficiencies or errors.
  6. Activity Frequency: Shows the number of times each business process activity is performed, helping to understand the workload and identify high-volume tasks.
  7. User Frequency: Indicates the count of process steps each user is involved in, aiding in balancing workloads and ensuring no single user becomes a bottleneck.
  8. Throughput Time vs. Total Waiting Time: Compares the average time tasks take to progress with the total delays experienced at each step, identifying and addressing bottlenecks.
  9. Process Step Rework Count: Counts the total number of times each process step is repeated, signaling potential issues such as incomplete data or errors.
  10. User Rework Count: Shows the cumulative count of rework instances by each user, revealing training needs or workload issues.
  11. Top 20 Cases with Longest Duration: Lists the top 20 process instances that took the longest to complete, revealing patterns or issues that contribute to delays.

 

Final Thoughts

SAP Process Mining has long been a valuable tool for organizations seeking to optimize operations. With Agentic AI and Generative AI in the picture, it is evolving from a diagnostic tool into a proactive, intelligent system that not only reports on your processes—but helps improve them, continuously.

Whether you are an SAP PMO, business analyst, or transformation lead, exploring this new frontier of autonomous process intelligence may be the key to unlocking the next level of operational excellence.

entry.title: Re: How to automate creation of Inbound delivery, GR, WT/...
entry.content:
Hi @Xiawei1 ,

to my knowledge your question refers to Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In order to automate such steps I'd propose to consider the following approach for a custom solution

  1. Utilize the Inbound Delivery Event to be notified of the creation of an inbound delivery
  2. Utilize the Warehouse Inbound Delivery API to post goods receipt
  3. Utilize the Warehouse Order and Task API to create the putaway warehouse tasks

Best regards,
Christian

Did you already execute streps from 2890778 - Special fields are not available in T-code FAGLL03 

CALL FUNCTION 'ITEM_STRUC_EXTENSION'
 EXPORTING
   BASIC_STRUCNAME          = 'FAGLPOSY'
   EXT_STRUCNAME            = 'FAGLPOSYEXT'
   EXT_FIELDS_TABNAME       = 'T021S'
   I_LSTCL                  = 'D'
   X_TRANSPORT              = ''.

SUBMIT balvbufdel...

entry.title: Re: Mass Data Management Support For Job Info
entry.content:
Hi @NehaMV13,

This is a very welcome enhancement, but two things would make it even better:

  1. Allow more than 50 records to be updated. This limit is far too low to make this very useful for us.
  2. Allow an option to update the latest sequence Job Info record if one already exists for the effective date selected, instead of adding yet another new record for that same date.

Are there any plans in these two areas for the future?

Regards,
Jay
